summary: 2026-06-27 18:26 PDT - The branch is docs/review-artifact only and the relative file links I checked exist, but the planning docs do not yet satisfy the story acceptance criteria. The roadmap still contains stale post-reconciliation claims, the execution plan is not DRY-linked to backlog anchors, and the open-work inventory is not fully truthful.

findings.title: Roadmap still contradicts shipped delete/export and multiplier state
findings.claim: The refreshed Phase 1 text says the backend data-rights routes already shipped, but the later Phase 1 data/API line still says to add DELETE/export endpoints. The same stale pattern remains in the cross-cutting privacy note at line 385, and the proposed `settings` model at line 397 still includes `multiplier` even though the branch says the multiplier was removed. This fails AC1's requirement to refresh phase notes that contradict current repo state.
findings.suggestion: Rewrite Phase 1 data/API as UI/privacy-page/deauth follow-up only, update the privacy note to say backend delete/export endpoints shipped and remaining work is UI/policy/deauth, and remove `multiplier` from the future `settings` model except as an explicitly ignored legacy field if needed.

findings.title: Execution plan rows are not linked to backlog anchors
findings.claim: AC5 requires every backlog item referenced in the plan to be linked to `reviews/backlog.md` or its review file by name/anchor, without duplicating full descriptions. Rows such as Client UI for data delete/export, Zoom deauthorization, In-Zoom client-error hardening, crash guards, ruleset-as-code, diagnostics cleanup, notetakers, and esbuild/Vite are plain bold text with copied tactical descriptions rather than stable backlog links.
findings.suggestion: Make each backlog-derived item name a markdown link to its exact `reviews/backlog.md` heading anchor or owning review file, and trim the item cell to sequencing metadata rather than copied tactical detail.

findings.title: One non-DONE backlog heading is silently excluded
findings.claim: The roadmap says the execution plan inventories every open unit of work from `reviews/backlog.md`, but the non-DONE backlog heading `Workflow skill defects — moved out of this repo` at `reviews/backlog.md:238` is not represented or named as excluded. AC2 says no open item may be silently dropped and deliberate exclusions must be named.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit exclusion row/note linking that backlog heading and saying it is excluded because it was moved out of this repo, or mark/reshape the backlog entry so it is no longer a non-DONE open project item.

findings.title: esbuild/Vite bump is carried as open though the repo already satisfies it
findings.claim: The execution plan lists `esbuild / Vite dev-only bump` as open work, but the current repo already has `client/package.json` on `vite` `^6.4.2`, `package-lock.json` resolves direct `vite` to `6.4.3`, and `esbuild` resolves to `0.25.12`, matching the backlog item's own done criteria. That makes the open inventory factually stale.
findings.suggestion: Mark the backlog item DONE and remove it from the execution plan, or rename the row to the actual remaining dependency concern with evidence from the manifest/lockfile.
